content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "How Many Paths?". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/1547/problem/G
constraints: 1 <= x, y, a, b <= n, m
x and y are the starting position
a and b are the ending position
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then for each test case: The first line contains two integers n and m, the number of rows and columns in the grid. The second line contains a string of length n, where each character is either '0' or '1', representing the grid. The third line contains two integers x and y, the starting position. The fourth line contains two integers a and b, the ending position.
content.outputFormat: The number of paths from the starting position to the ending position.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use dynamic programming to store the number of paths to each cell.
